summary: Lead started M6-SCENARIO-DATA-PIPELINE-001 after M6 diplomacy legitimacy closure entered origin/main. historical_researcher writer thread 019f07c1-17ed-7300-a467-cb7062d5064f is assigned to implement the three-scenario data pipeline within task allowed paths.

tests_run.result: Spawned historical_researcher writer thread 019f07c1-17ed-7300-a467-cb7062d5064f, nickname Chronicle.

decisions: Start M6-SCENARIO-DATA-PIPELINE-001 as the next dependency-ready M6 task after M6-DIPLOMACY-LEGITIMACY-001 closure entered origin/main.
Use explicit scenario schemas and deterministic manifest output; do not implement downstream diplomacy, legitimacy, succession, policy, event, map, encyclopedia, or victory systems in this task.
Formal cultural symbols, production art/audio/branding, telemetry, server behavior, and arbitrary-code mod support remain out of scope.
Do not modify PROJECT_STATUS.md or milestone/Gate docs because this task lists them as forbidden paths.
risks: R4 content pipeline task can accidentally encode unsupported formal historical claims; writer must label HISTORICAL, INFERRED, COMPOSITE, and FICTIONAL material and reject unlabeled or unsourced formal claims.
Pipeline references several not-yet-implemented Alpha surfaces; this task may validate references but must not implement those systems.
Content schema changes must remain explicit, deterministic, and compatible with content-runtime boundaries.
requested_action: Implement the three-scenario data pipeline within allowed paths, run required tests, and return a historical_researcher REVIEW handoff for independent qa_reviewer review.

acceptance_criteria: Three Alpha scenario data sets compile through explicit schemas with stable IDs, source or claim labels, dependency ordering, and deterministic manifest output.
Scenario records distinguish HISTORICAL, INFERRED, COMPOSITE, and FICTIONAL material and reject unlabeled or unsourced formal claims.
The pipeline validates references needed by diplomacy, legitimacy, succession, map candidates, policy, events, encyclopedia entries, and start-to-victory fixtures without implementing those systems.
No production art, audio, branding, cultural symbol lock, commercial release artifact, telemetry, server behavior, or arbitrary-code mod support is added.
required_tests: Content compatibility tests cover bad references, missing labels, unstable IDs, duplicate scenario keys, and deterministic manifest output.
